title: Sum of Two Numbers
description: Given two integers a and b, output their sum.
inputFormat: Two space-separated integers a b
outputFormat: Single integer (a + b)

title: Factorial
description: Compute n! for 0 ≤ n ≤ 12.
inputFormat: Single integer n
outputFormat: Single integer n!
solution:
import sys
n = int(sys.stdin.read().strip())
res = 1
for i in range(2, n+1):
    res *= i
print(res)

title: Reverse String
description: Given a string s, print it reversed.
inputFormat: Single line string
outputFormat: Reversed string

title: Find Maximum
description: Given an array of integers, find and print the maximum value.
inputFormat: First line: integer n (array size). Second line: n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: Single integer (maximum value)
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n = int(lines[0])
arr = list(map(int, lines[1].split()))
print(max(arr))

title: Count Vowels
description: Count the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u) in a given string. Case insensitive.
inputFormat: Single line string
outputFormat: Single integer (vowel count)
solution:
import sys
s = sys.stdin.read().strip().lower()
vowels = 'aeiou'
count = sum(1 for c in s if c in vowels)
print(count)

title: Palindrome Check
description: Check if a given string is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards). Ignore spaces and case.
inputFormat: Single line string
outputFormat: Print 'YES' if palindrome, 'NO' otherwise

title: FizzBuzz
description: For integers from 1 to n, print 'Fizz' if divisible by 3, 'Buzz' if divisible by 5, 'FizzBuzz' if divisible by both, otherwise print the number.
inputFormat: Single integer n
outputFormat: n lines of output

title: Array Sum
description: Calculate the sum of all elements in an array.
inputFormat: First line: integer n (array size). Second line: n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: Single integer (sum of all elements)
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n = int(lines[0])
arr = list(map(int, lines[1].split()))
print(sum(arr))

title: Even or Odd
description: Determine if a given integer is even or odd.
inputFormat: Single integer n
outputFormat: Print 'EVEN' or 'ODD'

title: String Length
description: Return the length of a given string (excluding newline).
inputFormat: Single line string
outputFormat: Single integer (length of string)
solution:
import sys
s = sys.stdin.read().strip()
print(len(s))

title: Two Sum Pairs
description: Given an array of n integers and a target value T, find all pairs of indices (i, j) where i < j such that arr[i] + arr[j] == T. Print each pair on a separate line as 'i j' (0-indexed). If no pairs exist, print 'NONE'.
inputFormat:
First line: two integers n and T.
Second line: n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: Each valid pair 'i j' on its own line in ascending order of i, then j. If none, print 'NONE'.

title: Frequency Counter
description: Given a string, count the frequency of each character and print them in alphabetical order. Print each character and its count separated by a space, one per line. Only count alphabetic characters (a-z), case insensitive.
inputFormat: Single line string
outputFormat: Lines of 'char count' sorted alphabetically by character
sampleOutput:
d 1
e 1
h 1
l 3
o 2
r 1
w 1
solution:
import sys
from collections import Counter
s = sys.stdin.read().strip().lower()
counts = Counter(c for c in s if c.isalpha())
for ch in sorted(counts):
    print(ch, counts[ch])

title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
description: Given an n×n matrix, compute the sum of both diagonals (main diagonal + anti-diagonal). If n is odd, the centre element must only be counted once.
inputFormat:
First line: integer n.
Next n lines: n space-separated integers each.
outputFormat: Single integer — the diagonal sum.
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n = int(lines[0])
matrix = [list(map(int, lines[i+1].split())) for i in range(n)]
total = 0
for i in range(n):
    total += matrix[i][i]
    total += matrix[i][n-1-i]
if n % 2 == 1:
    total -= matrix[n//2][n//2]
print(total)

title: Anagram Groups
description: Given n words, group them by their anagrams. For each group, print all words (sorted alphabetically within the group) on one line separated by spaces. Print the groups in lexicographic order of their first (sorted) word.
inputFormat:
First line: integer n.
Next n lines: one word each (lowercase letters only).
outputFormat: Each anagram group on its own line, words sorted and space-separated.
sampleInput:
6
eat
tea
tan
ate
nat
bat
sampleOutput:
ate eat tea
bat
nat tan
solution:
import sys
from collections import defaultdict
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n = int(lines[0])
groups = defaultdict(list)
for i in range(1, n+1):
    w = lines[i].strip()
    key = ''.join(sorted(w))
    groups[key].append(w)
for key in sorted(groups):
    print(' '.join(sorted(groups[key])))

title: Prime Sieve
description: Given an integer n, print all prime numbers from 2 to n (inclusive) in ascending order, one per line. If there are no primes in range, print 'NONE'.
inputFormat: Single integer n (2 ≤ n ≤ 10000)
outputFormat: Each prime on its own line, in ascending order. 'NONE' if no primes exist.

title: Stack Balanced Brackets
description: Given a string containing only '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the brackets are balanced. Print 'YES' if balanced, 'NO' otherwise.
inputFormat: Single line string of bracket characters
outputFormat: Print 'YES' or 'NO'

title: Longest Common Prefix
description: Given n strings, find the longest common prefix among all of them. If there is no common prefix, print 'NONE'.
inputFormat:
First line: integer n.
Next n lines: one string each.
outputFormat: The longest common prefix, or 'NONE' if it doesn't exist.

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array of n distinct integers and a target value T, find the index of T using binary search. If found, print the 0-based index. If not found, print -1.
inputFormat:
First line: two integers n and T.
Second line: n space-separated integers in strictly ascending order.
outputFormat: Single integer — the 0-based index, or -1 if not found.
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
first = lines[0].split()
n, T = int(first[0]), int(first[1])
arr = list(map(int, lines[1].split()))
lo, hi = 0, n-1
ans = -1
while lo <= hi:
    mid = (lo + hi) // 2
    if arr[mid] == T:
        ans = mid
        break
    elif arr[mid] < T:
        lo = mid + 1
    else:
        hi = mid - 1
print(ans)

title: Merge Sorted Arrays
description: Given two sorted arrays of integers, merge them into one sorted array and print it.
inputFormat:
First line: two integers n and m (sizes of the two arrays).
Second line: n sorted integers.
Third line: m sorted integers.
outputFormat: Single line of (n+m) sorted integers separated by spaces.
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n, m = map(int, lines[0].split())
A = list(map(int, lines[1].split()))
B = list(map(int, lines[2].split()))
merged = sorted(A + B)
print(' '.join(map(str, merged)))

title: Number Spiral
description: Given n, print an n×n matrix filled with integers 1 to n² in clockwise spiral order starting from the top-left corner.
inputFormat: Single integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 10)
outputFormat: n lines each containing n space-separated integers forming the spiral matrix.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Given an array of n integers, find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence (LIS).
inputFormat:
First line: integer n.
Second line: n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: Single integer — the length of the LIS.
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n = int(lines[0])
arr = list(map(int, lines[1].split()))
import bisect
tails = []
for x in arr:
    pos = bisect.bisect_left(tails, x)
    if pos == len(tails):
        tails.append(x)
    else:
        tails[pos] = x
print(len(tails))

title: Coin Change
description: Given a list of coin denominations and a target amount, find the minimum number of coins needed to make the exact amount. If it's not possible, print -1.
inputFormat:
First line: two integers n (number of denominations) and T (target amount).
Second line: n space-separated integer denominations.
outputFormat: Single integer — minimum coins needed, or -1 if impossible.
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n, T = map(int, lines[0].split())
coins = list(map(int, lines[1].split()))
dp = [float('inf')] * (T+1)
dp[0] = 0
for amount in range(1, T+1):
    for c in coins:
        if c <= amount and dp[amount-c] + 1 < dp[amount]:
            dp[amount] = dp[amount-c] + 1
print(dp[T] if dp[T] != float('inf') else -1)

title: Word Ladder Length
description: Given a start word and end word, and a dictionary of words, find the length of the shortest transformation sequence from start to end, where each step changes exactly one letter and every intermediate word must be in the dictionary. If no path exists, print 0. The start word is not required to be in the dictionary.
inputFormat:
First line: start word.
Second line: end word.
Third line: integer n.
Next n lines: one dictionary word each (all words same length, lowercase).
outputFormat: Single integer — length of shortest sequence (including start and end), or 0 if impossible.
sampleInput:
hit
cog
6
hot
dot
dog
lot
log
cog

testCases.input:
hit
cog
4
hot
dot
dog
cog

title: Minimum Path Sum Grid
description: Given an m×n grid of non-negative integers, find the minimum path sum from the top-left cell to the bottom-right cell. You may only move right or down at each step.
inputFormat:
First line: two integers m and n (rows and columns).
Next m lines: n space-separated non-negative integers each.
outputFormat: Single integer — the minimum path sum.

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Given an array representing heights of bars in a histogram (each bar has width 1), find the area of the largest rectangle that can be formed within the bars.
inputFormat:
First line: integer n.
Second line: n space-separated non-negative integers (bar heights).
outputFormat: Single integer — the maximum rectangle area.
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n = int(lines[0])
heights = list(map(int, lines[1].split()))
heights.append(0)
stack = []
max_area = 0
for i, h in enumerate(heights):
    start = i
    while stack and stack[-1][1] > h:
        idx, ht = stack.pop()
        max_area = max(max_area, ht * (i - idx))
        start = idx
    stack.append((start, h))
print(max_area)

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent land cells horizontally or vertically.
inputFormat:
First line: two integers rows and cols.
Next rows lines: cols space-separated characters ('1' or '0') each.
outputFormat: Single integer — number of islands.
solution:
import sys
from collections import deque
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
rows, cols = map(int, lines[0].split())
grid = [lines[i+1].split() for i in range(rows)]
visited = [[False]*cols for _ in range(rows)]
count = 0
for r in range(rows):
    for c in range(cols):
        if grid[r][c] == '1' and not visited[r][c]:
            count += 1
            q = deque([(r,c)])
            visited[r][c] = True
            while q:
                cr, cc = q.popleft()
                for dr, dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]:
                    nr, nc = cr+dr, cc+dc
                    if 0<=nr<rows and 0<=nc<cols and grid[nr][nc]=='1' and not visited[nr][nc]:
                        visited[nr][nc] = True
                        q.append((nr,nc))
print(count)

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Given a string s, find the length of its longest palindromic substring.
inputFormat: Single line string s (1 ≤ |s| ≤ 1000, lowercase letters only)
outputFormat: Single integer — length of the longest palindromic substring.
solution:
import sys
s = sys.stdin.read().strip()
n = len(s)
best = 1
def expand(l, r):
    while l >= 0 and r < n and s[l] == s[r]:
        l -= 1; r += 1
    return r - l - 1
for i in range(n):
    best = max(best, expand(i, i), expand(i, i+1))
print(best)

title: Subset Sum Count
description: Given an array of n positive integers and a target value T, count the number of distinct non-empty subsets whose elements sum exactly to T.
inputFormat:
First line: two integers n and T.
Second line: n space-separated positive integers.
outputFormat: Single integer — the total count of valid subsets.
solution:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n, T = map(int, lines[0].split())
arr = list(map(int, lines[1].split()))
dp = [0] * (T+1)
dp[0] = 1
for x in arr:
    for s in range(T, x-1, -1):
        dp[s] += dp[s-x]
print(dp[T])

title: Topological Sort
description: Given a directed acyclic graph (DAG) with n nodes (0-indexed) and m directed edges, print a valid topological ordering of the nodes. If the graph has a cycle, print 'CYCLE'.
inputFormat:
First line: two integers n and m.
Next m lines: two integers u v representing a directed edge from u to v.
outputFormat: Space-separated node indices in topological order, or 'CYCLE' if one exists.
solution:
import sys
from collections import deque
lines = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
n, m = map(int, lines[0].split())
adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
in_deg = [0] * n
for i in range(1, m+1):
    u, v = map(int, lines[i].split())
    adj[u].append(v)
    in_deg[v] += 1
q = deque(i for i in range(n) if in_deg[i] == 0)
result = []
while q:
    node = q.popleft()
    result.append(node)
    for nb in adj[node]:
        in_deg[nb] -= 1
        if in_deg[nb] == 0:
            q.append(nb)
if len(result) != n:
    print('CYCLE')
else:
    print(' '.join(map(str, result)))

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given n non-negative integers representing the heights of bars in an elevation map (each bar has width 1), compute how much rain water can be trapped between the bars.
inputFormat:
First line: integer n.
Second line: n space-separated non-negative integers (bar heights).
outputFormat: Single integer — total units of water trapped.
